{"key_event":"Dervish resistance amid fragmented colonial rule","reasoning":"Somali society retained substantial autonomous clan, customary-law, and religious networks capable of mobilizing resistance, especially through the Dervish movement, but organization was fragmented rather than nationally institutionalized. State power was low because no single authority governed Somalia: British and Italian rule was shallow and coastal, Ethiopian reach was contested, and Dervish authority was militarized but territorially limited.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":2.5}
